title: BotChain
subtitle: User-Defined Modular Robotics
info: Conceived and deployed OSI Layer 1 and 2 protocols in C++ for wired communication between ESP32-S3 microcontrollers, enabling real-time user-defined robotic control from a WiFi-connected remote PC
Implemented Manchester and NRZ-I encoding using the ESP32-S3 RMT API in C++, achieving >7 Mib/s average throughput with <1% packet corruption and <= 30 ms latency
Integrated Routing Information Protocol (RIP) for dynamic routing and automatic topology discovery, enabling plug-and-play networking for multiple robots
Built a Python wrapper for a custom C++ control library, significantly reducing development time and improving accessibility for end users
Realized a Unity UI to visualize robot topology, define motion sequences, and store them in ESP32 NVS for autonomous execution without a PC

Engineered a custom protocol for storing, reading, and exeucting preprogrammed movement sets, with Flatbuffers, to allow a robot to autonomously detect its own topology and send movement commands, eliminating the need for PC control
Added SX1276 LoRA transceiver support for long-range RC control, eliminating 2.4 GHz interference and enabling reliable extended-range operation

info.text: Presented at the University of Waterloo's 2026 ECE Capstone Symposium

links.label: University of Waterloo's 2026 ECE Capstone Symposium

title: Windows 2D Game Framework
subtitle: A C++ framework for building 2D games on Windows
info: Developed a custom game framework and rendering engine in C++ using Windows API, Windows GDI, and Direct2D, optimizing 2D graphics rendering and game mechanics
Leveraged Direct2D and Windows APIs to implement advanced rendering techniques, improving performance and visual fidelity across game scenes

title: Loo Programming Language
subtitle: A simple statically typed compiled programming language
info: Constructed a statically typed compiled programming language using C++, implementing a custom lexer, parser, and AST-based compiler backend to produce x86-64 assembly for Linux

Implemented key language features including control flow, typing rules, and expression evaluation; developed semantic analysis and code generation to guarantee correctness and performance of compiled programs

title: Real Time Operating System
subtitle: Creating a RTOS for an STM32 microcontroller
info: Built a RTOS for an STM32 microcontroller in C, implementing core OS components including a buddy dynamic memory allocator, EDF task scheduler, and SVC calls to enable kernel/user mode separation and syscall handling

Validated functionality and reliability by designing and running comprehensive unit and E2E tests in C, ensuring task scheduling correctness, memory safety, and stable system operation under edge cases

title: Verilog Memory System and Tester
info: Synthesized an emulated 256 B memory system and tester in Verilog, with 8 bit wide read/write address and data
Developed comprehensive functional testbenches to validate timing, data integrity, and edge-case behavior across synchronous read/write operations

title: VHDL Compiler
info: Developed a VHDL-to-logic-gate compiler in Java, translating VHDL source into logic gate diagrams (SVG) and corresponding bitstring representations—demonstrating compiler design, parsing, and digital logic synthesis skills
